findings.claim: Residual hedging asymmetry in the redesigned option set, but it points away from the key and is non-exploitable.
findings.evidence: A ('probably'), B ('may be why'), C ('likely') all carry an explicit epistemic hedge; the key D ('paid only when ships arrived safely, so keeping the lamps lit served their own interests') is the sole categorical, absolute-worded option ('only'). Under the standard test-wise heuristic (avoid absolutes / prefer the most cautiously-worded inference), a form-scanning student would be steered AWAY from D, not toward it.
findings.why: This does not restore blind-answerability toward the key — if anything it hardens the item against guessing — so it is not a defect. Recorded only for transparency since the redesign note explicitly claimed to kill a V-FINAL form-tell; the residual asymmetry is benign, not a leak. Item is still not blind-answerable by content: a student cannot know keepers were paid solely on safe arrival without reading the passage.
